Okay, now that we know all the different parts of the website, and there are more parts, I've just given you the ones that are basic tools, I want to actually show you how I wireframe a website. Recently, we created a story brand guide certification guide program, and we needed a sales page in order to sell our guide certification. So this was just literally a couple of weeks ago, and I sat down and I created a document that I could give to my designer that my designer could then turn into a website. That's basically what you'll be doing as a copywriter. You need to imagine and understand how the website will flow visually. The process usually works with me answering a few questions, and these are questions that you either need to ask about whatever product it is you're trying to sell, or you need to actually talk to your clients about. The first question is, what do we want this website to accomplish? So for me, I'm trying to educate people about guide certification, and of course, sell them on guide certification, right? So I know that's what I'm going to do. Then who is my target market? We actually have three targets, three segmented audiences for this product. One is somebody who wants to start a coaching business and actually be a marketing coach. Another is an existing coach, say an executive coach or a live coach who wants certification from story brands, so they can also add marketing consulting to their coaching business. And the third would be large corporations often want to certify people in-house to help them clarify their messaging. And so three different demographics, and I need to know that before I go in and you'll see how I message to that group of people. And then once I show you this entire document, then I'm going to show you the final product of what it looks like. But those are the two main questions. Then there's the third question, and this one is really important also. It's this, what passion, what desire do we want the person to have after they look at this website? And I want them burning to become a story brand certified guide realizing the incredible value and opportunity that's here. All right? To do that, I remember all the sections of a website that I have, and I just begin typing in actual sections before I actually type in any text. The first thing that we're going to want on this website is the header. And as you know, the thing that the header needs to do is it needs to pass the grunt test. So I'm looking for a very short bit of text that people can read in under a couple seconds and immediately get this feeling of, wow, there's a lot that's in this for me, and I know what I'm supposed to do, and I know exactly what the product is. It passes the grunt test. So I just came up with this text, the marketing coach everybody wants. And I'm imagining that in bold text in the header of this website. Why would I say the marketing coach everybody wants? Well, it's an identity piece, right? People want to be wanted. They want to be liked like others. So rather than just saying become a story brand certified coach, I actually say become the coach everybody wants so that when you walk in the room, people go, oh, here's the person who has the solution to our problem. I also need a little subhead there to explain exactly what it is. And so I'm just going to say become a story brand certified guide. And I know the top text will be big and bold. The bottom text will be more like a subtitle. And you know what? That's all I'm putting in my header. Now, of course, I'll have a call to action and apply now button, a transitional call to action, watch the video. But that's it. I don't want any more text in that header. Okay, now I've got some choices. I can put a value proposition here. I can put a cost here. A lot of times I like to put a positive in the header and then a negative right below the header. So something that says here's the pain that you're probably feeling. But for this particular website, didn't quite feel right. And this is where it gets subjective. You're going to have to become a little bit of an artist. We're teaching you the chords, but you've got to learn to play music. So you're going to kind of go feel it out. Now I could go negative with the next section. I think it would work fine, but I'm feeling something a little more positive. So I'm going to put in another section that is a value proposition. Then under the value proposition, I'm imagining this visually. So I'm imagining my designer is going to create some icons. And under those icons, we're going to spell out a little bit of text. And so this again, I'm appealing to the squirrel brain, the brain that is not reading a website, just scanning website. Because we're not at the point yet where they're ready to read a bunch of text. We just need to entice them a little bit. And so the three pieces of value proposition that I'm going to give them are the opportunities that exist for them in this program. And they can become a master marketing coach. They can add value to their existing coaching. And they can help clients clarify their message. So again, just in the first two sections of the website, very little text, but they know they can become the marketing coach everybody loves. They know they can do this by becoming a story brand certified guide. They know they have to apply. And they get three valuable benefits if they go through with this certification. Become a master marketing coach, add value to your coaching, and help clients clarify their message. I'm off to a really great start. Next, I'm actually going to include another value proposition. And yes, you can have multiple value, multiple costs, multiple costs to action. You can do anything you want once you know the chords. So the value proposition that I'm going to add is a little bit, three more icons that are a little bit more elaborated on. So I can actually include a little bit more text here. And I know because I know how my designer works, and I've done this enough that he's going to want a bold heading with a sentence underneath, a bold heading with a sentence underneath, a bold heading with a sentence underneath. So I'm going to give him the text that he needs. Now, I got to tell you, if this happens every time, my designer is unbelievable. His name is Kyle. I send him one of these documents. He creates the website. We throw the website up on a giant television screen. We call it a War Room Day. And we sit and we edit down that website. I almost always give my designer too much text. So keep this short. But my three points of value proposition, and these are really four, five, and six, because I've already given them three. Now I'm giving them three more. Join the best. Join our community of trained, organized, and equipped marketing consultants who are changing the world one business at a time. It's more than a career. It's a community. Strong felt need going out to the client there. Have an impact. Storybrand Framework helps companies grow fast. Becoming a certified storybrand guide ensures you'll deliver marketing advice that gets results for your clients. So now there's going to be results for the people that you work with, another value. Scale your coaching into a business or add storybrand certification to your repertoire of coaching deliverables. Either way, storybrand will increase your value in the marketplace. So three things that you get. Join the best, have an impact, and earn more money. And we're just in the second part of the website. It's a fantastic website so far. Quite proud of it, but let's keep going. Now we need an explanation paragraph. And again, people scan websites. They don't read them, but now we're to section four. And if they want to read, they can, but I'm going to do a little trick here. I'm going to give them one sentence and then a button or a link that just says continue reading and that will drop down the rest of these couple paragraphs so that they can either scan or read. I think the explanation paragraph is important. It does get your SEO ranked a little bit higher because you get those code words and searchable words in there. But also if it's an expensive product, people do want to read a little bit more. And when they read more, they feel like they're more educated and they've made a more educated decision, a less risky decision. So the paragraph is important. So let me show you what I've done for the paragraph for the Storybrand Certified Guide program. All right, let me just block in the text here and you can look. Here's my explanation paragraph. Whether you want to become a marketing consultant, enhance your value to your existing coaching business or add personal value within a large organization. Okay, let's stop there. What did I just do? I hit my three demographics. These are the three people I know who could use this product and will possibly buy it. I hit them right away. And then I say, the Certified Storybrand Guide program will get you where you want to go. And then you can click to hide the text or click to open up the text. But that's the only part that's going to be visible on the website unless you click to see more. Storybrand has quickly become one of the most trusted names in marketing. I'm giving myself authority there to position myself as the guide, created by bestselling author Donald Miller. Storybrand helps more than 3,000 business leaders clarify their message every year. Again, more and more authority and trust and positioning myself as the guide. And the results are incredible. Again, authority. Many businesses have reported doubling, tripling and even quadrupling their revenue while spending less, not more on their marketing efforts. Storybrand is certifying a select number of guides each year. It's actually true. We don't certify a ton of them. But I do want to present the scarcity to increase the value. But it's actually true. We don't certify a bunch. A certified Storybrand guide can consult with individual businesses to help them clarify their message and create a marketing strategy that works. Storybrand supports these guys with an immersive one-time training on-going digital resources and live on-going support. We will help you learn our framework, consult with businesses, and if you want, turn your consulting into an independent business. So there, it's all there in the paragraph. And to become a Storybrand certified guide, I even included the plan, one, fill out an online application, two, have a brief conversation with one of our representatives, three, receive your training and get to work building or enhancing your consulting business, and then at the bottom of this, a cost paragraph. Without a framework, you are just delivering marketing advice. But customers are looking for more. They're looking for a proven system they can trust to deliver results. Becoming a certified Storybrand guide is your path to delivering results for your clients while building the career of your dreams. And then, of course, a call to action. Apply to become a Storybrand certified guide today. Great. So now that's probably the hardest part is getting that paragraph tight. But it is your chance to elaborate on whatever product it is that you're trying to sell for your customer on this website.
